# Ramble 66

> The ultimate guide to traveling America's Mother Road for the Route 66 Centennial in 2026. Ramble 66 features an interactive map, trip planner, attraction database, hidden gems, Native American heritage sites, and weekly event coverage — all narrated by Big Bo Ramble.

## Key Pages

- [Interactive Map](https://ramble66.com/map): Explore every stop, attraction, and hidden gem along Route 66 from Chicago to Santa Monica.

- [Trip Planner](https://ramble66.com/planner): Plan a custom Route 66 road trip with personalized stops and routes.

- [Attractions](https://ramble66.com/attractions): Iconic roadside attractions, diners, motels, and landmarks along Route 66.

- [Hidden Gems](https://ramble66.com/hidden-gems): Off-the-beaten-path stops and secret finds along the Mother Road.

- [Native American Heritage](https://ramble66.com/native-american-sites): Indigenous cultural sites and heritage locations along Route 66.

- [Events Calendar](https://ramble66.com/events): Upcoming Route 66 festivals, car shows, and centennial events in 2026.

- [Blog](https://ramble66.com/blog): Stories, guides, and weekly digests about Route 66 travel and the 2026 centennial.

## About

Ramble 66 covers all eight Route 66 states: Illinois, Missouri, Kansas, Oklahoma, Texas, New Mexico, Arizona, and California. Content is focused on the 2026 Route 66 Centennial — the 100th anniversary of America's most iconic highway.
